message.content: Where does OpenClaw store its configuration and what key controls the active model?

content.text: OpenClaw stores its main configuration in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json. The key that controls the active model is agents.defaults.model.primary. For example, setting it to ollama/llama3.2 will make llama3.2 the model used by all agents unless overridden at the agent level. Fallback models are listed under agents.defaults.model.fallbacks as an array.

message.content: And what about the embedding model used for memory search?

content.text: The embedding model is configured separately under agents.defaults.memorySearch.model. This model is used specifically for generating embeddings when searching session memory. It should be an embedding-capable model such as nomic-embed-text or mxbai-embed-large, not a chat model.

message.content: Can I set a different workspace per agent?

content.text: Yes. The default workspace for all agents is set at agents.defaults.workspace. Individual agents can override this by setting their own workspace key at the agent level in openclaw.json. The workspace path determines where the agent reads and writes files during a session.
